title: Creating Folders
subtitle: Make directories with mkdir

sections.content: So far you've been exploring existing folders. Now it's time to **create your own**. The `mkdir` command makes a new empty folder.
sections.analogy: If your file system is a building, `mkdir` is adding a new room. You choose the name, and it appears ready to be filled.
keyPoints: Type `mkdir` followed by the folder name you want

sections.instruction: Let's build something. First, we need a folder for our project.
sections.prompt: Create a folder called `my-project`. Type `mkdir my-project`.

onSuccess.message: Folder created! Check the file explorer — you can see it appeared.

sections.prompt: List the contents to see your new folder.

sections.instruction: Now move into your new folder.

onSuccess.message: You're now inside your project folder.

sections.instruction: Create a folder inside for CSS files.
Now create one more for JavaScript files.

onSuccess.message: Both created. Check `ls` to see them.

sections.prompt: To create a new empty folder called `assets`, you type: ___ assets

completionMessage: You can now create folders with `mkdir`. Your project is taking shape!
